raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The artifact contains an explicit contribution claim ('We introduce VariantTalk') and an implicit gap statement; a review must assess whether the thesis and contribution framing are coherently and completely stated.","R1.2":"The pipeline has three sequential stages whose outputs must chain correctly; the opening paragraph's hedged F1 comparison must hold under its own stated caveats; cross-paragraph consistency between methodology and evaluation design warrants scrutiny.","R1.3":"The opening paragraph asserts F1 scores 'broadly comparable' to prior work while simultaneously acknowledging normalization-convention differences that complicate the comparison; the claim is materially weakened by the evidence presented in the same sentence.","R1.5":"Results (paragraph 1) appear before the contribution claim (paragraph 2) and methodology (paragraphs 3–4), producing an inverted narrative arc that a review should flag and potentially reorder.","R1.6":"Paragraph 1 contains a single heavily stacked hedge clause ('broadly comparable … arguably somewhat complicated … approximated as faithfully as the published descriptions appeared to permit') that severely impairs readability and is a clear target for prose revision."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review prioritize scientific rigor—making R1.2 (logical consistency) and R1.3 (evidence-claim alignment) load-bearing—or writing quality and structure—making R1.5 (structural reorganization) and R1.6 (writing polish) load-bearing?"}

rationale.R1.1: The artifact contains an explicit contribution claim ('We introduce VariantTalk') and an implicit gap statement; a review must assess whether the thesis and contribution framing are coherently and completely stated.
rationale.R1.2: The pipeline has three sequential stages whose outputs must chain correctly; the opening paragraph's hedged F1 comparison must hold under its own stated caveats; cross-paragraph consistency between methodology and evaluation design warrants scrutiny.
rationale.R1.3: The opening paragraph asserts F1 scores 'broadly comparable' to prior work while simultaneously acknowledging normalization-convention differences that complicate the comparison; the claim is materially weakened by the evidence presented in the same sentence.
rationale.R1.5: Results (paragraph 1) appear before the contribution claim (paragraph 2) and methodology (paragraphs 3–4), producing an inverted narrative arc that a review should flag and potentially reorder.
rationale.R1.6: Paragraph 1 contains a single heavily stacked hedge clause ('broadly comparable … arguably somewhat complicated … approximated as faithfully as the published descriptions appeared to permit') that severely impairs readability and is a clear target for prose revision.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize scientific rigor—making R1.2 (logical consistency) and R1.3 (evidence-claim alignment) load-bearing—or writing quality and structure—making R1.5 (structural reorganization) and R1.6 (writing polish) load-bearing?
